channel.title: Kristof Moeller — Blog
channel.description: Reflections on marine science, harmful algal blooms, IAEA capacity building, data analysis, and the science-policy interface.

item.title: The Same Data, Differently Seen — Why Your Choice of Visualisation Matters
item.description: Boxplots can be identical while hiding completely different data distributions. A practical guide to choosing visualisations that reveal rather than conceal.

item.title: From Pipette to Pipeline — How AI and Open-Source Tools Are Changing Lab Data Analysis
item.description: How combining R, Shiny, and AI-assisted development made it possible to automate a full competitive bioassay analysis pipeline.

item.title: ICHA 2025 — Harmful Algae Science at the Edge of the World
item.description: Reflections from the 22nd International Conference on Harmful Algae in Punta Arenas, Chile.

item.title: The JPO Programme — A Pathway Worth Knowing About
item.description: Background on the German JPO programme and reflections from the introductory seminar.

item.title: Building Common Ground — The INT7022 Workshop in Cuba
item.description: The INT7022 workshop in Varadero, Cuba brought together twenty countries to build consensus on harmonised HAB sampling and biotoxin analysis protocols.

item.title: A New Chapter — Joining the IAEA Marine Environment Laboratories
item.description: After four years at AWI Bremerhaven, I have joined the IAEA Marine Environment Laboratories in Monaco as JPO for Harmful Algal Blooms and Biotoxins.
